quranicRelevance: Blaming/censuring (Lawm), self-reproaching soul (Lawwamah - 'wa la oqsimu bin-nafsi al-lawwamah'), and blamed/disgraced (Maloomeen).

keyDerivations.meaning: To blame, censure, reproach, or find fault

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَلَا أُقْسِمُ بِالنَّفْسِ اللَّوَّامَةِ (75:2): And I swear by the self-reproaching soul (the active moral conscience).
لَا تَخَافُونَ لَوْمَةَ لَائِمٍ: They do not fear the blame of any critic.
discardedNoise: Obscure details of eye expressions in blame.
Post-classical legal definitions of public reprimands.
summaryEn: The root denotes blame and conscience, defining the active human conscience (Lawwamah), the fear of critics, and eschatological disgrace (Maloom).
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على اللوم والندم، محدداً النفس اللوامة اليقظة الأخلاقية (لوامة)، والجرأة أمام ملامة العواذل، والمذمة والمقت (ملوم).
rawText: أَلَامَ 4 He did a thing for which he should be blamed. (S in art. جنف, and L and TA in art. ريب.) تَلَوَّمَ 5 i. q. تَكَلَّفَ اللَّوْمَ. (Ham, p. 356.) لَائِمَةٌ A thing for which the doer is blamed. (TA.) لَامَ 1 , inf. n. لَوْمٌ, He blamed, censured, or reprehended, syn. عَذَلَ, (S, M, Msb, K,) a person, (S, Msb,) عَلَى كَذَا [for such a thing]. (S.)
